title: hoya odorata subsp. rizaliana
intro: Hoya odorata subsp. rizaliana, also known as Rizalian's Hoya or the Philippine Waxflower, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the Philippines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hoya odorata subsp. rizaliana include its potential to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
